oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Monitoring antimicrobial use in beef cattle . . . does it matter to practitioners?
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Schrag, Nora
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: clinical pharmacology
antimicrobial use
antibiotics
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: In 2023 Apley et al.1,2 examined the impact of metric selec­tion on antimicrobial use reporting and ranking across 14 U.S. beef feedyards from 2018-2019. The study analyzed 6,460 lots containing 936,660 head of cattle to understand how different calculation methods affect interpretations of antimicrobial use patterns. However, antimicrobial use measures without mea­sures of disease are not actionable for the practitioner. A study funded by ICASA is currently looking at the granular data from 8 feedyards in order to more accurately describe the disease context recorded along with the antimicrobial use. It is this dis­ease context data that has the greatest value to the practitioner.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 24-25
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
